description: This figure shows two scanning electron microscopy micrographs of Fe-Mn-Al-C lightweight steels subjected to 600 °C tempering treatment, each with a 20 μm scale bar. Subfigure (a) corresponds to 9Al-20Mn-1C alloy, while subfigure (b) corresponds to 9Al-30Mn-1C alloy. Both samples exhibit equiaxed austenite grains, with parallel grain striations of distinct orientations observed inside individual grains for both alloys. The 9Al-20Mn-1C sample in (a) displays continuous dark precipitate features along grain boundaries, while the 9Al-30Mn-1C sample in (b) has finer, discrete grain boundary carbides. As stated in the caption, the 9Al-20Mn-1C sample shows precipitates continuous with austenite grains, while 9Al-30Mn-1C only contains isolated carbides. This figure supports the conclusion that these striated microstructures do not produce the expected hardening effect from carbide precipitation, unlike the fine lamellar precipitate microstructure in lower Mn 9Al-10Mn-1C alloy, indicating potential detrimental effects on strength and ductility.
